target.profile_backing: criterion target eval/add_64k_f64_vec from fj-lax lax_baseline; hot path cloned large TensorValue inputs before arithmetic evaluation

one_lever.name: LiteralBuffer copy-on-write tensor element storage
one_lever.description: TensorValue.elements now stores Literal values in an Arc-backed LiteralBuffer. Cloning TensorValue shares the immutable element buffer; mutation APIs and mutable indexing call Arc::make_mut before changing elements.

baseline.command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-fj-core-literal-buffer-pass42-baseline-20260603 rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'eval/add_64k_f64' --sample-size 30 --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 3

after.command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-fj-core-literal-buffer-pass42-after-20260603 rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'eval/add_64k_f64' --sample-size 30 --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 3

isomorphism_proof.ordering: LiteralBuffer derefs to [Literal], implements slice iteration, indexing, and IntoIterator in Vec order; all existing loops still traverse elements in the same order.
isomorphism_proof.tie_breaking: No comparison, sorting key, or dispatch selection logic changed. Existing sort_by behavior delegates to Vec::sort_by on the same Literal sequence after copy-on-write.
isomorphism_proof.floating_point: Literal payloads, F64Bits/F32Bits encodings, NaN payloads, and signed-zero bit patterns are unchanged; the golden includes -0.0 and NaN payload preservation.
isomorphism_proof.rng: No RNG sources, seeds, or random ordering paths touched.
isomorphism_proof.serialization: LiteralBuffer serializes and deserializes as the same JSON array shape previously produced by Vec<Literal>.
isomorphism_proof.mutation_aliasing: Mutable operations call Arc::make_mut first, so mutating a cloned TensorValue preserves previous clone-by-value semantics.

validation.command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 ... rch exec -- cargo check -p fj-core --all-targets
RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 ... rch exec -- cargo check -p fj-lax --all-targets
RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 ... rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-core literal_buffer_semantic_golden -- --nocapture
RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 ... rch exec -- cargo check -p fj-ad --all-targets
RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 ... rch exec -- cargo check -p fj-dispatch --all-targets
validation.result: pass after one downstream to_vec adaptation

validation.command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 ... rch exec -- cargo check -p fj-ffi --all-targets
validation.result: pass after one marshal into adaptation

validation.command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 ... rch exec -- cargo check -p fj-py --all-targets
RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 ... rch exec -- cargo check -p fj-conformance --tests
RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 ... rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-core --all-targets -- -D warnings
RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 ... rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-lax --all-targets -- -D warnings
RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 ... rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'eval/add_64k_f64' --sample-size 30 --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 3

notes: The dense_ref row is retained as a run-local noise indicator. It moved from 32.959 us baseline on vmi1149989 to 58.058 us after on vmi1156319, so the raw target speedup is conservative relative to the normalized vec/ref ratio.
No BLAS, MKL, XLA, or unsafe code introduced.
